chapter: Chapter 2: Role of the Government in Health

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Health'?
questions.answer: Health means our ability to remain free of illness and injuries. Apart from diseases, it also includes getting clean drinking water and a pollution-free environment.

questions.question: What is the role of the government in a democracy regarding health?
questions.answer: In a democracy, people expect the government to work for their welfare. This can be through the provision of education, health, employment, and housing.

questions.question: Name some health facilities required to prevent and treat illnesses.
questions.answer: We need health centers, hospitals, laboratories for testing, ambulance services, and blood banks to prevent and treat illnesses.

questions.question: Who was Hakim Sheikh?
questions.answer: Hakim Sheikh was a member of the Paschim Banga Khet Mazdoor Samity (PBKMS), an organization of agricultural labourers in West Bengal.

questions.question: What happened to Hakim Sheikh in 1992?
questions.answer: In 1992, he accidentally fell off a running train and suffered head injuries. He was in a very serious condition and needed immediate treatment.

questions.question: Why did government hospitals refuse to admit Hakim Sheikh?
questions.answer: One hospital did not have a spare bed, and another did not have the specialized doctors or the necessary equipment needed for his treatment.

questions.question: Where was Hakim Sheikh finally treated?
questions.answer: He was finally admitted to a private hospital, where he received the necessary treatment. He had to spend a lot of money on his treatment.

questions.question: What is the Public Health Service?
questions.answer: The public health service is a chain of health centers and hospitals run by the government. They are linked together so that they cover both rural and urban areas.

questions.question: Why is the public health system called 'public'?
questions.answer: It is called public because the government has established these hospitals for all citizens and the resources to run them come from the taxes that we pay.

questions.question: What are Private Health Facilities?
questions.answer: Private health facilities are not owned or controlled by the government. Patients have to pay a lot of money for every service that they use in these facilities.

questions.question: What is the 'Cost of a Cure'?
questions.answer: In the story of Aman and Ranjan, it shows that while a private hospital is clean and fast, it is very expensive. A government hospital is cheaper but has long queues and less facilities.

questions.question: Mention one communicable disease spread through water.
questions.answer: Diarrhoea is a common communicable disease that is spread through contaminated water.

questions.question: What does a Village Health Centre usually have?
questions.answer: A village health centre usually has a nurse and a village health worker. They are trained in dealing with common illnesses.

questions.question: Who supervises the village health workers?
questions.answer: Village health workers work under the supervision of doctors at the Primary Health Centre (PHC).

questions.question: What is the function of District Hospitals?
questions.answer: At the district level is the District Hospital that also supervises all the health centres in the district.

questions.question: Why do people in rural areas have to travel long distances for health care?
questions.answer: Rural areas often lack specialized doctors and advanced medical equipment, forcing people to travel to cities for treatment.

questions.question: What is one reason why private health services are increasing in India?
questions.answer: Private services are increasing because they offer better facilities, specialized doctors, and faster service compared to government hospitals.

questions.question: Is adequate healthcare available to everyone in India?
questions.answer: No, adequate healthcare is not available to everyone. Private services are increasing but are expensive, and public services are often insufficient or overcrowded.

questions.question: What was the 'Kerala Experience' mentioned in the book?
questions.answer: In 1996, the Kerala government gave 40% of the state budget to Panchayats. This allowed them to plan and provide for water, food, and education properly.

questions.question: Which is considered one of the healthiest countries in Central America?
questions.answer: Costa Rica is considered to be one of the healthiest countries in Central America.

questions.question: What was the Costa Rican approach to health?
questions.answer: Costa Rica decided not to have an army and used that money to provide for the basic needs of the people, such as health, water, and sanitation.

questions.question: What are some barriers to healthcare for the poor?
questions.answer: Lack of money, the high cost of medicines, and the lack of proper transport to reach hospitals are main barriers for the poor.

questions.question: What is a 'Medical Tourist'?
questions.answer: Medical tourists are foreigners who come to India specifically for medical treatment at hospitals that offer world-class facilities at a lower cost than in their own countries.

questions.question: What does 'Public' mean in the context of government services?
questions.answer: Public refers to an activity or service that is meant for all people in the country and is mainly organized by the government.

questions.question: Why are poor people more likely to fall ill?
questions.answer: Poor people are more likely to fall ill because they are undernourished, do not have access to clean drinking water, and live in cramped conditions.
